$comment: ARM64 (AArch64) target descriptor (plan 13 AS3 + plan 12 D-ML7-2.1). 100% JSON-configured — the same target-blind LIR substrate that drives x86_64 reads this file. Adding ARM64 = drop this file; no C++ change. Encoding shape: `fixed32` (32-bit fixed word + bit-field slots). Calling convention: AAPCS64 (https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa).

target.name: arm64
target.description: ARM64 AArch64 — minimal opcode set + AAPCS64 calling convention + fixed32 byte-encoder. Cycle-3 scope: ret (0xD65F03C0) + mov reg-reg via ORR alias (0xAA0003E0 base + Rd + Rm).

$aggregateLayoutComment: FC6 (D-FF3-1): per-ABI layout params. `natural` (scalar align = byte size, capped) is the AAPCS64 + Apple arm64 rule; maxAlignment 16 = the AArch64 max fundamental alignment (128-bit vector / __int128 / long double).

opcodes.$comment: Slot 0 = the Invalid sentinel — same discipline as x86_64. Substrate's addInst guard rejects opcode 0 loudly.
Virtual-ISA pseudo-op — same role as x86's `arg`: 'this LirReg is the function's k-th input parameter'. ML7 callconv lowering rewrites to the real mov-from-arg-reg sequence.

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (FC7 C1c) — caller-side mirror of `arg`: 'this LirReg is the k-th return register of the preceding struct-returning call' (payload = per-class return ordinal). ML7 callconv lowering captures it from the return register. Declared for substrate uniformity; AAPCS64 struct returns land at C3, so no arm64 ret_piece is emitted yet.

AAPCS64 has no native `mov reg, reg` instruction — the canonical encoding is `ORR Xd, XZR, Xm` (32-bit fixed word 0xAA0003E0 = base + Rn=XZR=31; Rd and Rm via slots). LIR's `mov` is 2-operand (result + 1 source); 3-address natively (no requires2Address).

wires.$comment: ORR Xd, XZR, Xm — bits 16..20 hold Rm (the source register's encoding). The fixed word's bits 9..5 = 0b11111 (XZR) are baked in; the wire only adds Rm bits.

variants.$comment: MOVZ Xd, #imm16 — move a 16-bit UNSIGNED immediate (zeroing the rest of the register; hw shift = 0). Fixed word 0xD2800000 (sf=1, opc=10, fixed bits 100101, hw=00). The Imm16 slot writes imm16 at bits 5..20; Rd at bits 0..4. Selected by the `imm32` operand-kind guard (the engine's immediate-operand filter — the SLOT is 16-bit, MOVZ-specific). The entry trampoline uses this to load the exit-syscall number into x8 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64). A value wider than the 16-bit slot STILL fails loud at encode (A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange) — but the MIR->LIR const materialization now splits wide inline constants into the MOVZ + MOVK ladder BEFORE encode when the movk_lsl* family below is declared (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE, FC3.5 sweep-c3), so the encoder ceiling backstops hand-built LIR only.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE): MOVK Xd, #imm16, LSL #16 — keep-other-bits 16-bit insert at bits 31:16. ARM ARM C6.2.227: sf [31] | opc=11 [30:29] | 100101 [28:23] | hw [22:21] | imm16 [20:5] | Rd [4:0]; X-base 0xF2800000, hw=01 -> 0xF2A00000. The MIR->LIR const materialization emits the MINIMAL ladder: MOVZ #chunk0 (zeroing all 64 bits) + one MOVK per NONZERO higher 16-bit chunk — selected by constant magnitude at the LOWERING (an operand's VALUE cannot guard encoding variants), capability-probed by mnemonic presence (x86's imm32 mov form never consults this family). requires2Address: the ISA reads Xd's other bits — the LIR shape is result==operand[0] (the accumulating register), wire index 0 lands in Rd (the 2-addr dest-wire satisfies the result-placement validate rule). ONE deliberately width-ABSENT X-form variant: the ladder is seeded by MOVZ (which zeroes bits 63:32), so the X-MOVK is value-correct for width-32 constants too (chunks 2/3 are never emitted at width 32) — the W-ORR zext / xor_rdx_zero width-invariance precedent.

MOVK Xd, #imm16, LSL #32 — hw=10 -> 0xF2C00000 (X-only: sf=0 reserves hw>=10). Chunk 2 of the wide-immediate ladder; emitted only for width-64 constants whose bits 47:32 are nonzero (sign-extended negative int32 patterns).
MOVK Xd, #imm16, LSL #48 — hw=11 -> 0xF2E00000 (X-only). Chunk 3 of the wide-immediate ladder.
AAPCS64 RET (X30 implicit) = 0xD65F03C0. No operands at LIR level; the link register x30 is implicit per the calling convention.
Hard-trap. AArch64 BRK #0 = 0xD4200000. Cycle-3 includes for completeness so a synthesized LIR can terminate without depending on `ret` semantics.

ARM64 binary ops (AS3b, all 3-address natively — no requires2Address). Fixed-word base patterns: ADD reg-reg = 0x8B000000; SUB = 0xCB000000; AND = 0x8A000000; ORR = 0xAA000000; EOR = 0xCA000000. Wires: Rd at 0..4 (dest), Rn at 5..9 (src1), Rm at 16..20 (src2).

encoding.$comment: FC3 c2 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS): the reg-reg shape is width-keyed 64/32 — the W-form is the SAME shifted-register ADD word with sf (bit 31) CLEARED (ARM ARM C6.2.5: a W-register write zeroes the upper 32 bits of the X register, the C-defined unsigned-int wraparound for free). The imm12 shape stays width:64-only — its sole producer is the callconv sp-adjust (Ptr-width); a width-32 add-imm inst would match NOTHING and fail loud rather than silently compute with a 64-bit carry.

variants.$comment: ADD Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x0B000000 (the X-form 0x8B000000 with sf=0).

ADD Xd, Xn, #imm12 (immediate). Fixed word 0x91000000 (sf=1, op=0 add, S=0, sh=0). imm12 at bits 10..21 via the Imm12 slot; Rd 0..4, Rn 5..9. Used by the callconv prologue/epilogue stack adjust (`add sp, sp, #frame`). 2-operand LIR form [reg, imm]: operand 0 → Rn (also the dest via resultSlot), operand 1 → Imm12. Range 0..4095.
FC3 c2: SUB Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x4B000000 (the X-form 0xCB000000 with sf=0).

SUB Xd, Xn, #imm12 (immediate). Fixed word 0xD1000000 (sf=1, op=1 sub, S=0, sh=0). imm12 at bits 10..21; Rd 0..4, Rn 5..9. Used by the callconv prologue stack adjust (`sub sp, sp, #frame`). Same 2-operand [reg, imm] LIR form as ADD-immediate. FC3 c2: width:64-only like add's imm12 shape (sole producer is the Ptr-width sp-adjust; a width-32 sub-imm fails loud).

opcodes.$comment: MUL Xd, Xn, Xm = MADD Xd, Xn, Xm, XZR. Base = 0x9B000000 | (XZR=31 << 10) = 0x9B007C00. Slots: Rd, Rn, Rm (Ra=XZR baked into fixedWord).

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: MUL Wd, Wn, Wm = MADD Wd, Wn, Wm, WZR — 0x1B007C00 (the X-form 0x9B007C00 with sf=0; the W product IS the C unsigned-int product mod 2^32).

opcodes.$comment: FC1 V2-4.X (2026-06-10): NEG Xd, Xm = SUB Xd, XZR, Xm (shifted-register alias, Rn=XZR baked). Base = 0xCB000000 | (XZR=31 << 5) = 0xCB0003E0. Slots: Rd, Rm. Surfaced by the modulo corpus' negative literals — under the BASELINE (no-ConstFold) pipeline a unary minus reaches LIR as a live `neg`, and arm64 had never declared one (every prior arm64 corpus avoided unary minus).

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: NEG Wd, Wm = SUB Wd, WZR, Wm — 0x4B0003E0 (the X-form 0xCB0003E0 with sf=0).

opcodes.$comment: FC1 V2-4.X (2026-06-10): SDIV Xd, Xn, Xm — data-processing (2 source), sf=1. Base = 0x9AC00C00 (sf=1 | S=0 | 11010110 | Rm[20:16] | opcode 000011[15:10] | Rn[9:5] | Rd[4:0]). The MIR→LIR div lowering's Rule-1 NATIVE realization: result-bearing 3-address op, no implicit registers, no pre-op (contrast x86 cqo+idiv_op). ARM64 SDIV never traps — divide-by-zero yields 0 and INT_MIN/-1 wraps (A-profile), so the C-UB divide cases are non-trapping on this target. SMod has no hardware instruction: the lowering realizes rem = n - (n/d)*d via sdiv + the fused `msub` (D-LIR-MOD-MSUB-FUSION ✅ closed FC3.5 sweep-c3; the generic mul+sub expansion remains the fallback for targets without a fused op).

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: SDIV Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x1AC00C00 (the X-form 0x9AC00C00 with sf=0; ARM ARM C6.2.250 data-processing 2-source).

opcodes.$comment: FC1 V2-4.X (2026-06-10): UDIV Xd, Xn, Xm — base = 0x9AC00800 (opcode field 000010 vs SDIV's 000011). Unsigned divide; same Rule-1 native shape as sdiv. UMod expands via udiv+mul+sub.

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: UDIV Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x1AC00800 (the X-form 0x9AC00800 with sf=0).

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-LIR-MOD-MSUB-FUSION): MSUB Xd, Xn, Xm, Xa = Xa − Xn·Xm — data-processing (3 source), ARM ARM C6.2.230: sf [31] | 00 [30:29] | 11011 [28:24] | 000 [23:21] | Rm [20:16] | o0=1 sub [15] | Ra [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0]. X-base = 0x9B000000 | (1<<15) = 0x9B008000 (the MADD base the `mul` alias builds on, with o0=1). The MIR→LIR rule-3 remainder expansion PREFERS this fused form when declared: msub r, q, d, n = n − q·d in ONE instruction (operand 0 = quotient → rn, operand 1 = divisor → rm, operand 2 = dividend → ra), replacing the generic mul+sub pair. First consumer of the fixed32 `ra` slot (bits 10..14).

variants.$comment: MSUB Wd, Wn, Wm, Wa — 0x1B008000 (the X-form 0x9B008000 with sf=0; the W result IS the C 32-bit remainder mod 2^32).

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c1 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS shifts residue): LSLV Xd, Xn, Xm — logical shift left, variable. Data-processing (2 source) like sdiv/udiv: sf [31] | 0 0 [30:29] | 11010110 [28:21] | Rm [20:16] | opcode 001000 LSLV [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C6.2.215) = base 0x9AC02000. NATIVE 3-address — no implicit registers, no count pin (contrast x86's CL contract); the MIR->LIR shift lowering's native arm emits ONE op. The hardware masks the count: Xm mod 64 (X-form) / Wm mod 32 (W-form, sf=0 = 0x1AC02000) — C declares counts >= width UB, so no masking is emitted. CONSTANT counts also ride this form (the count materializes through the const mov like any operand); the LSL-immediate alias (UBFM) is a peephole-class refinement, deliberately NOT declared this cycle.

variants.$comment: LSLV Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x1AC02000 (sf=0; count mod 32).

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c1: LSRV Xd, Xn, Xm — LOGICAL shift right, variable (MIR LShr; zero-fill, unsigned operands). Same data-processing (2 source) shape; opcode field 001001 = base 0x9AC02400 (ARM ARM C6.2.217). W-form 0x1AC02400.

FC3.5 sweep-c1: ASRV Xd, Xn, Xm — ARITHMETIC shift right, variable (MIR AShr; sign-fill, signed operands). Opcode field 001010 = base 0x9AC02800 (ARM ARM C6.2.21). W-form 0x1AC02800. DSS defines C's implementation-defined negative >> as sign-fill on every target — same contract as x86 SAR.
ORR Xd, Xn, Xm = 0xAA000000 base (sf=1, opc=01, fixed pattern). NOT the same as the mov-alias which bakes Rn=XZR into the fixed word — this is the general-purpose 3-address OR.

D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64 (ARM64 control-flow): materialize a 0/1 GPR from the NZCV flags by condition — the AArch64 analog of x86 `setcc`. ARM64 has no `setcc`; the canonical idiom is `CSET Xd, <cond>` = `CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, invert(cond)` (when the INVERTED condition is FALSE — i.e. the requested condition is TRUE — the false-arm increments XZR→1; otherwise 0). Single fixed word base 0x9A9F07E0 (2594113504): sf=1, CSINC opcode, Rm=XZR=31<<16, Rn=XZR=31<<5, op2=01, Rd at bits 0..4. The cond nibble is placed at bits 12..15 (condBitPos=12) and INVERTED (condInvert=true) from the inst payload via condCodeFromPayload + the schema's condCodeEncoding table. LIR shape mirrors x86 setcc: result=value, ZERO value operands (reads FLAGS), cond from payload. lowerICmp emits `cmp` then `setcc(cond)` then `zext`. VERIFY: `cset x0, gt` = GT(0xC) inverted to 0xD at bits 12..15 = 0x9A9FD7E0.

Zero-extend a 32-bit value to 64-bit — AArch64 `MOV Wd, Wm` = `ORR Wd, WZR, Wm` (a 32-bit register op auto-zeroes the upper 32 bits of the X register, so a W-form ORR IS the W→X zero-extend). Single fixed word base 0x2A0003E0 (704644064): sf=0 (32-bit), ORR opc=01, Rn=WZR=31<<5, Rd at bits 0..4, Rm (source) at bits 16..20 via the one reg wire. Mirrors x86_64's `zext` LIR shape: result=value, ONE reg operand. The natural consumer is lowerICmp's setcc→zext pair (a clean 0/1 r64); also reused by MIR ZExt. FC3.5 sweep-c1 (D-CSUBSET-ZEXT-32-TO-64): the variant stays deliberately WIDTH-ABSENT (match-any — the xor_rdx_zero precedent) because this ONE word is the correct realization for BOTH source shapes the MIR→LIR gate admits: a Bool 0/1 register AND a full U32 (the W-write zeroes bits 63:32 regardless). x86 needs two width-keyed forms (movzx byte vs mov r32,r32); arm64's realization is width-invariant. VERIFY: zext of w1→x0 (Rd=0, Rm=1) = 0x2A0103E0.

FC3 c2 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS): NEW opcode — sign-extend a 32-bit value to 64-bit. AArch64 `SXTW Xd, Wn` = the SBFM alias `SBFM Xd, Xn, #0, #31`: word 0x93407C00 = sf=1 [31] | opc=00 [30:29] | 100110 [28:23] | N=1 [22] | immr=000000 [21:16] | imms=011111 [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C6.2.263 SXTW / C6.2.234 SBFM). Mirrors x86_64's `sext` (movsxd r64, r/m32) LIR shape: result=value, ONE reg operand on the Rn slot. The MIR→LIR SExt gate admits I32 sources only — exactly this form's inherent width. First consumer: the LP64 `int` → `unsigned long` UAC conversion in the datamodel_runtime_width corpus.

variants.$comment: width 32: SXTW Xd, Wn (SBFM Xd,Xn,#0,#31 = 0x93407C00) — I32→I64. Width-keyed (was width-absent) now that the byte form below is declared — convergence-fix D forbids mixing a match-any sibling with a width-keyed one. The MIR→LIR SExt arm threads widthFlagsForType(source): an I32 source → width 32 → here.

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): SXTB Xd, Wn = SBFM Xd, Xn, #0, #7 — sign-extend the low BYTE to 64 bits (the x86_64 `movsx r64,r/m8` peer). Word 0x93401C00 = the SXTW word with imms 31→7 (bits 15:10): sf=1 | opc=00 | 100110 | N=1 | immr=0 | imms=7 | Rn | Rd (ARM ARM C6.2.* SBFM/SXTB). Selected when the SExt source type is Char (width 8 on the LIR inst) — the char→int widening. Reads exactly the low byte (the char), so the source register's upper bits (zero-extended by the LDURB byte load) are irrelevant.

opcodes.$comment: FC3 c2 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS): NEW opcode — truncate a 64-bit value to 32 bits. AArch64 `MOV Wd, Wm` = `ORR Wd, WZR, Wm` (0x2A0003E0 — sf=0, Rn=WZR baked; the SAME word the `zext` opcode uses, because the ISA-level operation is identical: a W-register write zeroes the upper 32 bits). Two mnemonics deliberately exist over one word — each conversion mnemonic carries its INHERENT width pair (trunc: 64→32 mod-2^32, C's conversion semantics; zext: Bool/byte→64) and the MIR→LIR gates police each independently. The variant is WIDTH-KEYED 32 like x86's trunc: a Trunc to 16/8 bits reaching the assembler matches NOTHING and fails loud.

Allocate a stack-frame slot (virtual / pseudo-op — NO encoding, exactly like x86_64's `alloca`). Payload = spill-slot/local index threaded verbatim from MIR Alloca. Result is a pointer-class virtreg holding the slot's effective address. The ML7 callconv pass (lir_callconv) materializes this into a frame-relative `lea result, [sp + offset]` once the frame layout is known — target-blind, and arm64 already has `lea` (ADRP+ADD), so no encoder change is needed; this declaration just lets MIR Alloca lower on arm64. A function with body locals (`int n = 42;`) emits one alloca per local.

Direct AND indirect call, one mnemonic with per-callee-kind variants (FC4 c2). Direct call to a symbol — AArch64 `BL imm26` (branch-with-link); indirect call through a register — AArch64 `BLR Xn` (branch-with-link-to-register). Declared under the engine's GENERIC `call` verb (NOT the ISA spellings 'bl'/'blr'), the same convention every other opcode in this file already follows (`mov`=ORR-alias, `add`=ADD, `xor`=EOR, `or`=ORR): the target's mnemonics ARE the engine's verb vocabulary; the real ISA mnemonics live in this comment. This is what lets the format-blind trampoline emitter (`opcodeByMnemonic("call")`) and the MIR→LIR `MnemonicSlot::Call` resolve the call on ANY target — and keeping BLR a VARIANT (not a second opcode) keeps isCall / functionHasCalls / caller-saved-clobber semantics shared by construction. BL fixed-word base = 0x94000000 (opcode bits 26..31 = 0b100101); imm26 wire at bits 0..25 emits a call26 relocation, bits left at 0 (linker patches at link time). BLR fixed-word base = 0xD63F0000 with the callee register in the Rn slot (bits 5..9): BLR X9 = 0xD63F0120.

variants.$comment: FC4 c2: BLR Xn — 0xD63F0000 | Rn<<5 (indirect call through a register). fixedWord 3594452992 = 0xD63F0000; sanity precedent: the shipped ret row's 0xD65F03C0 = 3596551104 (RET X30, same D6 family).

opcodes.$comment: AArch64 SVC #0 = 0xD4000001 — supervisor call (the syscall instruction). Zero-operand at LIR level; the Linux AArch64 syscall ABI puts the syscall number in x8 (the format's processExit.syscallNumGpr), args in x0..x7, return in x0. Mirrors x86_64's `syscall` (0F 05). Added at D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 so the entry trampoline can issue the exit_group syscall and terminate the process. fixedWord 3556769793 = 0xD4000001 (SVC with imm16=0).

Spill-reload pseudo-op (target-blind, NO encoding — exactly like x86_64's). Payload = spill-slot index. The ML7 callconv pass materializes this into the target's real `load` (the LDUR opcode below) ONLY when a function actually spills / restores a callee-saved reg; the spill-rewrite pass requires the mnemonic to EXIST upfront. A register-pressure-light leaf (e.g. `return 42`) never emits one.

Spill-store pseudo-op (target-blind, NO encoding). Payload = spill-slot index. Operand[0] = the physical reg being stored. Materialized into the target's real `store` (the STUR opcode below) by the ML7 callconv pass, only when a function spills / saves a callee-saved reg.

Memory LOAD — AArch64 LDUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (unscaled, signed 9-bit byte offset). Fixed word 0xF8400000 (size=11 64-bit, opc=01 load, unscaled mode bits 11..10 = 00). Operand layout (shared LIR load form): [base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result. Wires: result→Rt (rd, bits 0..4), base_reg(index 0)→Rn (bits 5..9), MemOffset(index 2)→Imm9 (bits 12..20, signed). The MemBase(index 1) carries no AArch64 bits (scale must be 1; the walker validates it). The callconv materializes frame_load into this. Offset range -256..255; a wider frame fails loud (scaled LDR imm12 = future).

variants.$comment: width 64: LDUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (0xF8400000). Width-keyed (was width-absent) now that the byte form below is declared — convergence-fix D forbids mixing a match-any sibling with a width-keyed one. Every GPR integer/pointer load carries width 64 (memAccessWidthFlags returns 0 ⇒ width 64 for non-Char GPR types).

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): LDURB Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0x38400000 — the LDUR word with size 11→00). Loads ONE byte, ZERO-extending it into Wt (and a W-write zeroes bits 63:32 of Xt) — a char/byte memory access MUST be byte-EXACT, a 64-bit LDUR of a 1-byte string/array element reads 7 bytes past it. ZERO-extend is the signedness-AGNOSTIC byte fetch (the x86_64 movzx peer); sign semantics live in the following SXTB cast (char→int), which re-reads the low byte. Same operand layout as the 64-bit form. Selected when the Load's result type is Char (width 8 via memAccessWidthFlags).

opcodes.$comment: Memory STORE — AArch64 STUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (unscaled). Fixed word 0xF8000000 (opc=00 store). Operand layout (shared LIR store form): [value_reg, base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)], no result. Wires: value_reg(index 0)→Rt (rd, bits 0..4), base_reg(index 1)→Rn (bits 5..9), MemOffset(index 3)→Imm9 (bits 12..20, signed). MemBase(index 2) carries no bits (scale must be 1). The callconv materializes frame_store into this.

variants.$comment: width 64: STUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (0xF8000000). Width-keyed (was width-absent) now that the byte form below is declared — convergence-fix D forbids mixing a match-any sibling with a width-keyed one. Every GPR integer/pointer store carries width 64 (memAccessWidthFlags returns 0 ⇒ width 64 for non-Char GPR types).

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): STURB Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0x38000000 — the STUR word with size 11→00). Writes EXACTLY the low byte of Wt — a 64-bit STUR of a 1-byte element clobbers 7 neighbours. Signedness-agnostic (only the low 8 bits are written). Same operand layout as the 64-bit form. Selected when the stored value's type is Char (width 8 via memAccessWidthFlags).

opcodes.$comment: Load-effective-address — AArch64 ADRP+ADD pair (D-AS4-3 multi-instruction macro-op materializing a module symbol's address into Xd). Mirrors x86_64's 1-operand RIP-relative `lea reg, [rip+sym]` so `lowerGlobalAddr` is target-blind: ONE SymbolRef operand wired into BOTH words. word0 = ADRP Xd, sym@PG (0x90000000=2415919104): Rd at bits 0..4; immlo[30:29]+immhi[23:5] left ZERO — the adr_prel_pg_hi21 reloc patches the ±4 GiB page delta. word1 = ADD Xd, Xd, #:lo12:sym (0x91000000=2432696320, the ADD-imm base shared with `add`'s imm variant): Rd at 0..4 AND Rn at 5..9 (BOTH = the result reg via extraResultSlots — ADD reads its own dest as the source base); imm12[21:10] left ZERO — the add_abs_lo12_nc reloc patches. Both relocs target the SAME symbol at byte offsets base+0 and base+4. The two `sym.patch` wires write NO immediate bits (the linker owns them); they carry only relocationKind + wordIndex.

variants.$comment: FRAME-RELATIVE lea — `lea Xd, [base + #disp]` = AArch64 `ADD Xd, Xn, #imm12`. This is the form the ML7 callconv's `emitFrameAddr` materializes from `alloca` (a body-local's effective address): 3-op no-index shape [base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result, MIRRORING x86_64's 3-op `lea` (so the callconv stays target-blind — it emits the same operand layout for both ISAs). Single fixed word 0x91000000 (2432696320, the ADD-imm base, shared with `add`'s imm variant + the SymbolRef lea's word1): base_reg→Rn (bits 5..9), result→Rd (bits 0..4), MemOffset→Imm12 (UNSIGNED, bits 10..21; frame offsets are non-negative). MemBase carries no AArch64 bits (scale must be 1; the walker validates it via membase.noscale). A frame offset >4095 fails loud at the walker (A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange) — the shifted ADD imm12<<12 form for larger frames is a future generalization (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE, parallel to the unscaled-vs-scaled LDR/STR split).

BASE+INDEX lea (D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA) — `lea Xd, [base + index*1 + 0]` = AArch64 `ADD Xd, Xn, Xm` (shifted-register add, LSL #0). The 4-op form `lowerGep` emits for an indexed pointer access `p[n]` ([base_reg, index_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp=0)]), MIRRORING x86_64's 4-op SIB lea so the GEP stays target-blind (closing the gap that made `p[n]` fail loud on arm64 for int AND char alike). The GEP emits scale=1 + disp=0 ALWAYS today, so this single ADD is the complete realization: base→Rn (5..9), index→Rm (16..20), result→Rd (0..4); MemBase→membase.noscale (scale MUST be 1, fail-loud) and MemOffset→memoffset.zero (disp MUST be 0 — the register-add form has NO displacement field, and bits 10..21 overlap Rm so an Imm12 wiring would corrupt it; fail-loud on nonzero). Fixed word 0x8B000000 (the ADD reg-reg base, shared with `add`'s reg-reg variant). A scale!=1 (typed-array Gep scaling by sizeof(T)) needs the shifted form `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm,LSL #log2(scale)` — a FUTURE consumer (D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE, co-design with ML6 address-mode synthesis), NOT built speculatively.

opcodes.$comment: D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26 (ARM64 conditional control-flow): integer compare writing the NZCV flags, no GPR result. AArch64 `CMP Xn, Xm` is an alias for `SUBS XZR, Xn, Xm` (subtract-and-set-flags discarding the result into the zero register). Fixed word 0xEB00001F = SUBS (sf=1, op=1, S=1) with Rd=31 (XZR) baked in; Rn at bits 5..9, Rm at bits 16..20. Mirrors x86's `cmp` LIR shape (InvalidLirReg result, 2 reg operands) — ML5 lowerICmp + lowerCondBr emit it before the conditional branch. NO resultSlot (the flags are the implicit output). Reuses the Rn/Rm slots already exercised by the add/sub reg-reg variants.

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: CMP Wn, Wm = SUBS WZR, Wn, Wm — 0x6B00001F (the X-form 0xEB00001F with sf=0). A U32 compare MUST read 32 bits — a bitcast-from-negative operand carries sign-extended upper bits the X-form would misread.

FC3.5 sweep-c1: CMP Xn, #imm12 = SUBS XZR, Xn, #imm12 — add/subtract (immediate), ARM ARM C6.2.395: sf=1 [31] | op=1 sub [30] | S=1 [29] | 100010 [28:23] | sh=0 [22] | imm12 [21:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd=XZR=31 [4:0] = 0xF100001F. FIRST consumer: the MIR->LIR non-fused CondBr arm's `cmp cond, #0` over a PHI-produced Bool (a short-circuit `&&`/`||` result used as an if/while condition cannot fuse with its compare). x86's cmp had its reg-imm form from the start; this arm64 gap was masked until the S2 marker fix let logical-op conditions reach codegen. Reuses the Imm12 slot (bits 10..21) the callconv's sub-sp frame adjust already exercises; the walker range-checks 0..4095 fail-loud.
FC3.5 sweep-c1: CMP Wn, #imm12 — the X-form with sf=0: 0x7100001F. The 32-bit sibling per the FC3-c2 width axis (a U32-typed Bool-ish compare reads 32 bits).

opcodes.$comment: D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26: unconditional intra-function jump. AArch64 `B <label>` (encoding 0x14000000, opcode bits 26..31 = 0b000101). The 26-bit imm field (bits 0..25, SCALED by 4) is the SIGNED word displacement to the target block — resolved at ASSEMBLE time via the Imm26 slot + per-function patch list (NOT a linker relocation; the same Imm26 slot is symbol-bearing for the BL/`call` form, but a BlockRef operand here selects the intra-function path). Operand[0] is a BlockRef naming the target basic block. Mirrors x86's `jmp` (E9 rel32). Imm26's ±128 MiB reach covers any single function.

D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26: conditional intra-function jump with mandatory fallthrough. 2-WORD macro: `B.cond <ifTrue>` (word 0) then `B <ifFalse>` (word 1) — the AArch64 analog of x86's `0F 8x rel32; E9 rel32` compound. word0 = B.cond base 0x54000000 (opcode bits 24..31 = 0b01010100, bit 4 = 0); the cond nibble (bits 0..3) comes from the inst's payload (TargetCondCode) via `condCodeFromPayload` + the schema's `condCodeEncoding` table; the 19-bit signed PC-relative offset (bits 5..23, scaled by 4) goes to the Imm19 slot → ifTrue block. word1 = B base 0x14000000 with the Imm26 slot → ifFalse (fallthrough) block. Both displacements resolve at assemble time (BlockRef operands, no relocation). operand[0]=ifTrue (taken when cond holds), operand[1]=ifFalse. A future optimizer elides word1 when ifFalse is the next-laid-out block (D-OPT-JCC-FALLTHROUGH). Mirrors the x86 jcc wiring + the lea multi-word fixedWords+wordIndex shape.

D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FADD Dd, Dn, Dm — scalar double, FP data-processing (2 source). Base 0x1E602800 = 0 0 0 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 double [23:22] | 1 [21] | Rm [20:16] | opcode 001010 FADD [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2.*). 3-operand NATIVE like sdiv — deliberately NO requires2Address (x86's fadd carries it for ADDSD's 2-address shape; declaring it here would force a pointless extra fmov through the legalize pass). FC3.5 sweep-c2 (D-CSUBSET-F32-CODEGEN): the F32 sibling FADD Sd, Sn, Sm (ftype=00 → base 0x1E202800) rides the width axis — D-form keyed 64, S-form keyed 32.

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FADD Sd, Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E202800.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: FDIV Dd, Dn, Dm — scalar double divide, FP data-processing (2 source), opcode 000110 at [15:10]: base 0x1E601800 (ftype=01); F32 sibling FDIV Sd, Sn, Sm = 0x1E201800 (ftype=00) — ARM ARM C7.2 FDIV (scalar). 3-operand NATIVE (no requires2Address). First consumer: NaN construction (0.0/0.0) for the D-COND-FLOAT-NAN-TRUTHINESS-FCMP corpus witnesses; closes the fdiv stub on this target.

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FDIV Sd, Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E201800.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (FCmp LIR lowering): FCMP Dn, Dm — floating-point compare writing NZCV, no register result (the float sibling of `cmp`). Base 0x1E602000 = 000 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 [23:22] | 1 [21] | Rm [20:16] | op=00 [15:14] | 1000 [13:10] | Rn [9:5] | opcode2=00000 [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2 FCMP; opcode2 bit4=1 would be FCMPE, the signaling form — DSS does not model the FP environment, so the quiet FCMP serves all predicates; the NZCV comparison RESULTS are identical). F32 sibling FCMP Sn, Sm = 0x1E202000 (ftype=00). NZCV outcomes: equal → Z=1(C=1); less → N=1; greater → C=1; unordered → C=1,V=1 — the truth table the float condCodeEncoding arms below realize (full derivation at mir_to_lir floatCmpPlan).

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FCMP Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E202000.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: float↔float precision change for MIR FPTrunc/FPExt — FCVT (scalar): 000 11110 [31:24] | type [23:22] = SOURCE type | 1 [21] | 0001 [20:17] | opc [16:15] = DEST type | 10000 [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2 FCVT). The width axis keys on the SOURCE (the MIR→LIR FPTrunc/FPExt arm threads a widthOverride from the source operand's type — the result-width default would select exactly the WRONG direction): F32 source → FCVT Dd, Sn (type=00, opc=01 → 0x1E22C000, the FPExt widen); F64 source → FCVT Sd, Dn (type=01, opc=00 → 0x1E624000, the FPTrunc narrow).

variants.$comment: F64 source → FCVT Sd, Dn (narrow to single).
F32 source → FCVT Dd, Sn (widen to double).

opcodes.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FCVTZS Xd, Dn — double to signed 64-bit integer, round toward zero (FP→integer conversion). Base 0x9E780000 = sf=1 [31] | 00 | 11110 [28:24] | ftype=01 double [23:22] | 1 [21] | rmode=11 toward-zero [20:19] | opcode=000 FCVTZS [18:16] | 000000 [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0]. The result is GPR-class via the MIR integer type (regClassFor) — rd carries the X destination, the rn wire the D source; the 5-bit slots are class-blind hwEncodings, exactly the GPR-dest/FPR-src split x86's cvttsd2si makes through modrm.reg/modrm.rm. MIR FPToSI is the producer. FC3.5 sweep-c2: the F32-source sibling FCVTZS Xd, Sn (ftype=00 → 0x9E380000) rides the SOURCE-width axis (the FPToSI arm threads the source's width — the X destination is unchanged).

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FCVTZS Xd, Sn — ftype=00 single source, base 0x9E380000.

opcodes.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FMOV Dd, Dn — FP register-register move, double. Base 0x1E604000 = 0 0 0 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 [23:22] | 1 [21] | opc=000000 FMOV [20:15] | 10000 [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (FP data-processing 1 source — the single source rides Rn, NOT Rm; contrast the GPR mov's ORR alias whose source is Rm). Bound to the fpr class's `move` slot in registerClassOps[] — consumed by MIR value/phi copies and the callconv arg/return moves whenever the register class is FPR (the arm64 analog of x86's movaps role; a D-form copy moves the full 64-bit view, no upper-lane-merge hazard to dodge). FC3.5 sweep-c2 adjudication: register COPIES stay width-BLIND (variant deliberately width-absent, match-any) — the D-form move preserves an F32 value exactly (it lives in the low 4 bytes; S[n] is the low half of D[n]), the same reasoning as x86's full-register MOVAPS serving both widths. Only memory accesses (fldur/fstur) are width-exact.

D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FPR memory LOAD — LDUR Dt, [Xn, #simm9] (SIMD&FP 64-bit, unscaled). Fixed word 0xFC400000 = the GPR LDUR 0xF8400000 with V=1 (bit 26): size=11 [31:30] | 111 [29:27] | V=1 [26] | 00 [25:24] | opc=01 load [23:22] | 0 [21] | imm9 [20:12] | 00 [11:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rt [4:0]. Operand layout mirrors the GPR `load` row exactly (shared LIR load form [base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result): result→Rt (rd), base→Rn, MemOffset→Imm9 (signed -256..255), MemBase carries no bits (membase.noscale). Bound to the fpr class's `load` slot in registerClassOps[] — the callconv epilogue saved-d-reg reload, stack-resident F64 args, and MIR Load of an F64 rodata global (the literal-materialization path) all resolve here. F64-only: the F32 S-form (size=10, 0xBC400000) lands with an F32 consumer.

encoding.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (D-CSUBSET-F32-CODEGEN): width-keyed pair — LDUR Dt (8-byte, 0xFC400000, width 64) vs LDUR St (4-byte, size=10 → 0xBC400000, width 32; ARM ARM C7.2 LDUR SIMD&FP). lowerLoad threads the loaded type's width for FPR-class results: an 8-byte D-load of a 4-byte F32 rodata item would read past the item.

variants.$comment: FC3.5: LDUR St, [Xn, #simm9] — size=10 single, base 0xBC400000 (4-byte load).

opcodes.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FPR memory STORE — STUR Dt, [Xn, #simm9] (SIMD&FP 64-bit, unscaled). Fixed word 0xFC000000 = the GPR STUR 0xF8000000 with V=1 (bit 26); opc=00 store. Operand layout mirrors the GPR `store` row exactly (shared LIR store form [value_reg, base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)], no result): value→Rt (rd wire), base→Rn, MemOffset→Imm9 (signed). Bound to the fpr class's `store` slot in registerClassOps[] — the callconv prologue saved-d-reg spill is the first consumer (AAPCS64 declares d8-d15 callee-saved + regalloc prefers callee-saved, so the FIRST FPR vreg in any function needs this store — the exact shape that surfaced x86's movsd_store on ms_x64).

encoding.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: width-keyed pair — STUR Dt (8-byte, width 64) vs STUR St (4-byte, 0xBC000000, width 32). lowerStore threads the stored VALUE's type width for FPR-class stores; the regalloc spill path stays width-default 64 over 8-byte slots (value-preserving for both float widths).

variants.$comment: FC3.5: STUR St, [Xn, #simm9] — size=10 single, base 0xBC000000 (4-byte store).

$condCodeEncodingComment: D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64 (mirror of x86's table): per-cond-code nibble for the AArch64 B.cond instruction's bits [3:0]. Numeric values are the AArch64 condition codes (ARM ARM C1.2.4): EQ=0, NE=1, CS/HS=2, CC/LO=3, HI=8, LS=9, GE=10(0xA), LT=11(0xB), GT=12(0xC), LE=13(0xD). Signed comparisons map slt→LT(0xB)=11, sle→LE(0xD)=13, sgt→GT(0xC)=12, sge→GE(0xA)=10; unsigned map ult→CC/LO=3, ule→LS=9, ugt→HI=8, uge→CS/HS=2. The encoder ORs this nibble into word 0 bits 0..3 of the jcc macro (vs x86's low 4 bits of the opcode byte — same field-position abstraction, different ISA placement). The 10 integer arms are REQUIRED; the FC3.5 float arms (next comment) are per-entry optional.
$condCodeEncodingFloatComment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (FCmp lowering — D-COND-FLOAT-NAN-TRUTHINESS-FCMP): float condition arms over the FCMP NZCV truth table (ARM ARM: equal → Z=1,C=1; less → N=1; greater → C=1; unordered → C=1,V=1). fogt = GT (Z=0∧N=V, 0xC — unordered has N=0≠V=1 → false ✓; NOTE the integer-ugt HI=8 would be TRUE on unordered, a NaN miscompile — this is why float codes are separate); foge = GE (N=V, 0xA); foeq = EQ (Z=1, 0 — unordered Z=0 → false ✓); fune = NE (Z=0, 1 — TRUE on unordered, exactly C 6.5.9 `!=`); fuo = VS (V=1, 6 — after FCMP the overflow flag IS the unordered predicate); ford = VC (7). fone is deliberately ABSENT: NE is true on unordered, and no single AArch64 cond is `ordered ∧ unequal` — its absence signals the composed realization (One = cset-NE ∧ cset-VC), the mirror of x86's foeq/fune absence. Olt/Ole need no entries — the lowering swap-canonicalizes them to fogt/foge (MI/LS would also realize them directly; the swap keeps ONE target-blind rule).
$registersComment: AAPCS64 general-purpose registers x0..x30 + zero-register XZR (encoded as 31 in source operands; uses register's hwEncoding). Stack pointer `sp` is also encoded as 31 but in the SP-slot context — hwEncoding=31 with class GPR is shared between XZR and SP at the byte level. D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): the SIMD&FP file is declared as d0..d31 (class fpr) — the V registers' 64-bit D views, sharing the same 5-bit field encoding.

registers.$comment: Stack pointer shares hwEncoding=31 with XZR at the byte level (the context determines which: SP-using addressing modes vs ZR-encoding instructions). The substrate treats them as distinct names with distinct ordinals; the JSON-side ordinal is what matters for the cc lookup.
registers.name: sp

registers.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): the V-register file's 64-bit D views, class fpr. FC3.5 sweep-c2: F32 values live in the S views (the low 32 bits of the same registers) — the width axis on the instruction VARIANTS selects D vs S forms; the register declarations stay the D views (the 5-bit hwEncoding is shared by every view, and copies/spills move the containing D view, value-preserving for F32). widthBytes=8 is the D view, NOT the full 128-bit V register — the q/v views (and their subOf aliasing) are the future F128/SIMD cycle's work. hwEncoding = the 5-bit register number every SIMD&FP instruction form shares.
registers.name: d0

$registerClassOpsComment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): per-register-class move/load/store mnemonics — the same FC2 Part B table x86_64 declares. A class WITHOUT a row uses the universal defaults (mov/load/store) iff it is gpr — the substrate default class; every other row-less class FAILS LOUD at the consumer (L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing), because the class-blind alternative is a silent miscompile (a GPR ORR/LDUR against a D-register hwEncoding assembles to valid-looking words that move the WRONG register file). fpr: move=FMOV(D), load=LDUR(D), store=STUR(D).
$callingConventionsComment: AAPCS64 (https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa/blob/main/aapcs64/aapcs64.rst). Integer args: x0..x7. FP args: d0..d7 (D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE 2026-06-10 — declared with the FPR substrate). Caller-saved: x0..x18 (incl. x16/x17 = IP0/IP1; PLT-clobberable) + v0-v7/v16-v31 in their d views. Callee-saved: x19..x28 + x29 (frame ptr) + x30 (link reg) + v8-v15 low 64 bits (d8..d15).

callingConventions.name: aapcs64
callingConventions.$callerSavedFprComment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): AAPCS64 §6.1.2 — v0-v7 (args/results) and v16-v31 are caller-saved; declared here in their d views. Being listed in callerSaved is what keeps the regalloc honest about them: an allocatable register NOT in callerSaved is treated as call-safe (lir_regalloc buildFreeLists), so omitting these while argFprs makes them allocatable would let a float live across a call sit unsaved in a volatile d-register — the same reason x86_64's sysv row lists all sixteen xmm registers here.
callingConventions.$calleeSavedFprComment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): AAPCS64 §6.1.2 — v8-v15 must be preserved by a callee ONLY in their LOW 64 BITS; the top 64 bits are caller's responsibility everywhere. The d8..d15 rows are therefore EXACTLY the preserved contract for F64 (the prologue's D-form STUR saves precisely the bits the ABI guarantees — the same low-64-only shape as Win64's xmm6-15/MOVSD). CAVEAT for a future F128/SIMD cycle: a Q/V-register value live across a call must NOT rely on these rows — the callee preserves only the low half; re-examine when q/v views land.
callingConventions.$entryStackPointerBiasComment: D-LK10-ENTRY-TRAMP-PROLOGUE: AArch64 BL does NOT push a return address (it writes LR / x30 directly), so process entry — whether by kernel-jump (Linux/Mac) or `RtlUserThreadStart`-BL (Windows) — leaves SP 16-byte-aligned (bias 0 mod 16). Trampoline `sub sp, 0` is emitted as nothing — `alignedSizeWithBias(shadowSpaceBytes=0, stackAlignment=16, bias=0) = 0`.
callingConventions.$callPushBytesComment: D-LK10-ENTRY-ML7-FRAME-BIAS-UNIFY: AArch64 BL writes the return address into LR/x30 — no stack push. callPushBytes=0 is the right ISA-level fact for ARM64. Non-leaf functions on ARM64 still save x30 in their prologue + reload it before `ret`; because x30 is the `linkRegister` (deliberately NOT in `calleeSaved` — it must not be allocatable), this is realized by the link-register fold in `lir_callconv.cpp::materializeOneFunc` (gated on hasCalls + cc.linkRegister), which adds x30 to `savedRegs` so it lands in `savedRegAreaSize`. Without it the epilogue `ret` returns to a clobbered LR — see D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER. Independent of the call-push bias.

callingConventions.$comment: apple_arm64 — Apple's ARM64 ABI for Apple platforms (developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/writing-arm64-code-for-apple-platforms). The ABI catalog (abi_catalog.cpp) maps (arm64, MachO) → CcApple → "apple_arm64"; shipping this row CLOSES D-FF3-5 (trigger: 'apple_arm64 cc row ships in arm64.target.json') and is what lets `resolveAbi` + the entry trampoline resolve a Mach-O-ARM64 cc. The general-purpose REGISTER vocabulary is IDENTICAL to AAPCS64 — integer args x0..x7, returns x0/x1, callee-saved x19..x28+x29, link x30, sp, 16-byte stack alignment, bias 0 (BL writes LR, no push). The Apple ABI's documented DIVERGENCES from AAPCS64 are NOT register-assignment differences: (1) variadic args are ALWAYS passed on the stack (vs AAPCS64's register-then-stack) and (2) a different indirect-result-location rule. Neither is modeled at today's scalar-only asm tier (variadic support is gated at D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE), so for the scalar register-passing this row carries the same fields as aapcs64; a SEPARATE named row keeps the ABI identity explicit and correct, so when variadic/indirect-result lands it is updated HERE, not by silently reusing aapcs64. The always-stack variadic rule + indirect-result-location divergence are pinned at D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE (they land WITH the proper CcApple field set when the trigger fires — a variadic OR struct-return call on arm64-Apple — not as a silent aapcs64 reuse; distinct from D-FF3-4, which is the Microsoft CcMSARM64 split).
callingConventions.name: apple_arm64
callingConventions.$fprComment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): the SIMD&FP register vocabulary is IDENTICAL to AAPCS64 (Apple's documented divergences are variadic/indirect-result rules, not register assignment): args d0..d7, return d0, v0-v7/v16-v31 caller-saved, v8-v15 callee-saved in their LOW 64 BITS only (d8..d15 — same F128/SIMD-cycle caveat as the aapcs64 row's $calleeSavedFprComment).

$relocationsComment: AArch64 ELF relocation kinds (plan 13 AS4 + plan 14 LK6 cycle 1 + D-LK6-1 closure 2026-06-01). The 'formula' field is a closed-enum discriminator (RelocFormulaKind in target_schema.hpp): 'aarch64_call26' = (S+A-P)>>2, signed-26-bit, OR into BL/B inst[25:0]; 'aarch64_adr_prel_pg_hi21' = ((S+A)>>12)-(P>>12), signed-21-bit, ADRP-split into immlo[30:29]+immhi[23:5]; 'aarch64_add_abs_lo12' = (S+A)&0xFFF, ADD imm12[21:10]; 'linear' (used for abs64) writes 8 LE bytes. Tag values are arbitrary per-target — only the NAME round-trips with format.json cross-reference. Paired anchors: D-LK6-8 (ARM64 PLT stub bytes) + D-LK6-14 (Apple Silicon chained-fixups dispatch) remain open and are independent of the formula reshape.
